title: Roblox Q1 2026: Revenues Rise Ahead of Age-Gating and Short-Term Bookings Headwinds Forecast
text: Q1 2026 showed strong results with revenue rising 39% year-over-year to $1.442 billion and bookings increasing 43% to $1.731 billion. However, the outlook has become considerably more cautious. Roblox now expects full-year 2026 bookings to land between $7.33 billion and $7.6 billion, down significantly from its prior projection of $8.28 billion to $8.55 billion. The updated guidance calls for full-year bookings growth of only 8–12% and acknowledges that safety initiatives, recommendation experiments, and a Russia platform ban are weighing on user growth and bookings in the near term. Daily active users grew 35% to 132 million and engagement increased 43% to 31 billion hours. Strong growth was driven by international expansion, with rapid expansion in Japan (160% growth), India (110% growth), and Indonesia (more than 700% growth). The trouble started in January, when Roblox became the first major online gaming platform to require age verification just to access its chat feature. Roblox expects DAUs to contract further between Q1 and Q2, then return to sequential growth in Q3, with that confidence resting on seasonal tailwinds, planned product updates, and the upcoming launch of age-based accounts in June.
